skill.name: url-citation-search
skill.description: Find papers and preprints that cite a given URL, especially blogs, docs pages, project pages, or other web content that standard citation indexes often miss. Use when the user provides a URL and wants confirmed citing works, evidence from PDF or HTML references, DOI or arXiv links, BibTeX, or a deduplicated citation report.
skill.summary: Search academic sources to find verified papers and preprints that cite any given URL.
skill.author: zjsxply

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 27 static findings from 1 file (71 lines). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES: 25 backtick findings are Markdown code span formatting, not Ruby shell execution. The 2 weak crypto findings are a false flag on the YAML description field. The skill is a legitimate URL citation search assistant with no malicious intent. Minor concern: the skill instructs the AI to fetch external web pages, but this is inherent to its citation-searching purpose.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 25 instances of Markdown code span backticks as Ruby shell execution. All are standard Markdown formatting (e.g., `arxiv.org`, `citation_reference`, `confirmed`). No actual shell commands exist in this file.

content.user_title: Find academic papers that cite any URL
content.value_statement: Researchers often need to find which academic papers cite a blog post, documentation page, or project website. This skill searches multiple academic sources and verifies each citation by inspecting PDF and HTML references, so you get confirmed results instead of search engine guesses.
actual_capabilities: Search for academic papers that cite a specific URL across multiple sources
Verify citations by inspecting PDF and HTML reference lists
Distinguish direct citations from mirror and official variant citations
Generate deduplicated citation reports with DOI and arXiv links
Provide BibTeX output for citing papers
Handle blog posts, documentation, project pages, and other non-standard web content
limitations: Cannot access paywalled or login-protected academic databases
PDF text extraction may miss some citations due to formatting
Limited to sources accessible through standard web search
Cannot verify citations behind server-side rendered paywalls

use_cases.title: Track citations of your research blog post
use_cases.description: A researcher published a blog post about their methodology and wants to find which papers have cited it.

use_cases.title: Track citations of open-source project documentation
use_cases.description: A developer wants to find academic citations of their open-source project documentation pages.

use_cases.title: Build literature review citations
use_cases.description: A student needs to find papers that cite a specific project page for their literature review section.

prompt_templates.title: Basic URL citation search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find papers that cite this URL: https://example.com/blog/post
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick citation check for a single URL

prompt_templates.title: Detailed citation report
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all papers citing https://example.com and give me a deduplicated citation report with DOI or arXiv links for each.

prompt_templates.title: BibTeX output request
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for works citing https://example.com/research-page and return BibTeX entries for all confirmed citing papers.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-source verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Check which papers cite https://example.com/project. Verify each citation by checking both PDF reference lists and HTML metadata. Distinguish direct citations from mirror citations.

output_examples.output:
I found 3 papers that cite https://example.com/research:

1. Smith et al. (2024) - Advances in Machine Learning
   Published in NeurIPS 2024
   DOI: 10.1234/example.2024.01
   Evidence: Direct citation in HTML reference list

2. Johnson and Lee (2023) - A Survey of Modern Approaches
   arXiv: 2312.56789
   Evidence: URL found in PDF reference section
   Status: Preprint (no published version found)

3. Garcia et al. (2024) - Practical Applications
   Published in ICML 2024
   Evidence: Mirror citation (cited the GitHub mirror URL instead of canonical)

output_examples.input: Check which papers cite https://example.com/docs and give me BibTeX
output_examples.output:
I found 2 confirmed citing papers:

1. @article{chen2024framework,
  title={A New Framework for Data Processing},
  author={Chen, Wei and Patel, R.},
  journal={ACM Computing Surveys},
  year={2024},
  doi={10.1234/csur.2024.02}
}

2. @inproceedings{martinez2024scalable,
  title={Scalable Approaches to Data Management},
  author={Martinez, A. and Thompson, K.},
  booktitle={Proceedings of VLDB 2024},
  year={2024},
  arxiv={2401.12345}
}

best_practices: Provide the exact URL you want to search for to get the most accurate results
Specify if you want a brief list, detailed report, or BibTeX output to match your workflow
Review the evidence notes to understand whether each citation is direct or from a mirror
anti_patterns: Do not assume a paper cites a URL just because it covers the same topic - require visible reference evidence
Do not rely only on search snippets for verification - always inspect the actual reference list
Do not skip mirror and variant URLs - papers often cite older or alternative versions of the same page

faq.question: What types of URLs can this skill search for?
faq.answer: Any web URL including blog posts, documentation pages, project pages, GitHub repositories, and demo sites.

faq.question: How does this differ from Google Scholar cited-by counts?
faq.answer: This skill searches for URL citations specifically, which standard citation indexes often miss. It also verifies each citation manually.

faq.question: Can it access paywalled papers?
faq.answer: No. The skill works with freely accessible sources through web search and open access repositories.

faq.question: Does it support BibTeX export?
faq.answer: Yes. You can request BibTeX output for all confirmed citing papers in your query.

faq.question: How does it verify citations?
faq.answer: It checks PDF reference lists, HTML metadata, and bibliography pages for the exact URL before confirming a citation.

faq.question: What does direct versus mirror citation mean?
faq.answer: A direct citation uses the exact URL you provided. A mirror citation uses an alternative version like a different domain or mirror site.
